note: Verbatim phrases of the locked narration, in spoken order, split at the script's break tags and sentence/colon boundaries into caption-sized pieces. Never rewritten, summarized, or reordered. Block windows are MEASURED onsets (normalized cross-correlation of each block mp3 against the joined master, 8 kHz mono): the nominal Sigma(audio+0.4) formula using ffprobe per-file durations overshoots the ffprobe-probed master by 0.514s (> 0.3 tolerance) because the joined master pads each block boundary with ~0.36s of silence, not the full 0.4s the formula assumes; measured onsets drift monotonically from 0 at s01 to -0.519s at s15. ffprobe master duration 260.833s (audioDurationS).

phrases.text: Open Analytics.
This is the deepest sidebar in the platform.
Twenty-two pages.
Do not try to memorize them one at a time.
Learn the four buckets they fall into, and any page becomes easy to find.
The labels down the left are your map.
First bucket, Analytics.
Ten pages, and these are the live dashboards:
the whole network at a glance, each location, memberships, downsells, retail.
One catch worth flagging now.
The item named Marketing up here is a single dashboard, not the Marketing group further down.
Same word, two places.
The module drops you on the Executive Dashboard.
You met this one last lesson: the period toggles up top, the location scope right beside them.
We are not stopping.
Today is about learning what lives all around it.
Click Downsells.
When a member goes to cancel, the system offers a downgrade to keep them instead.
These six cards are the scoreboard for that fight:
how many tried to leave, how many you talked into staying, and the dollars you kept.
Right now, you are saving better than four in ten.
Scroll down for the why.
The reasons people cancel, ranked, and the offers that win them back.
Remember the Command Center flagging members with downsell signals?
This is where that play cashes out.
Read the top reason, read the save rate, and you know in ten seconds whether the flow is working.
Second bucket, Marketing.
Five pages, and the feel changes.
These are reports, not dashboards.
Campaign results, who responded, which offers landed,
where new members came from, and who you managed to win back.
Open Campaign Performance.
A summary band on top, a sortable table underneath.
Look at the header.
No location picker this time.
Instead, Filters and Export.
That is the tell for a report: you slice it, sort it, and pull it into a spreadsheet.
Every page in this group works the same way.
Third bucket, Intelligence.
Six pages, and this is the analyst's shelf:
revenue trends, industry benchmarks, churn prediction, lifetime value, and your online reputation.
These are the heavier questions, the ones you dig into rather than glance at.
Start at B-I Overview.
Eight cards, and together they read the health of the whole business:
recurring revenue, retention, churn, lifetime value, even your net promoter score.
When someone asks how the network is really doing, this is the single screen you open.
Keep scrolling.
A full year of revenue, your plan mix, and the members quietly drifting toward the exit.
At the bottom, the Deep Dives.
Picture B-I Overview as the front door,
and the other five Intelligence pages as the rooms it opens into.
Last bucket, Forecasting.
Just one page this time.
Ten under Analytics, five under Marketing, six under Intelligence, one here.
That is your twenty-two, and now the whole sidebar fits in your head.
Click Forecasting.
This is the only page that looks forward instead of back.
Four numbers set the scene:
next month's projected revenue, how close you are running against plan for the year,
and how accurate the model has been.
See the New Record button?
We leave that one alone.
Below, the workhorse.
A revenue projection, the forecast broken out by location, and an accuracy readout.
Then the Forecast List itself:
every period lined up with what you predicted, what actually came in, and the gap between the two.
Read one line and it clicks.
Take May of last year: forecast around two hundred thirty-eight thousand,
actual came in near two forty-two, off by a point and a half.
That is a closed month.
The pending row shows dashes, because its real numbers are not in yet.
Hands off New Record.
We are only reading.
So that is the map.
Four buckets.
Analytics for the dashboards, Marketing for the reports,
Intelligence for the deep questions, Forecasting for what comes next.
Twenty-two pages, and you can reach any of them from the sidebar, or jump straight there with Search.
Learn the buckets, and you never hunt for a page again.
